title: Motion Verbs: jít, jet, chodit, jezdit
description: Master the four essential Czech motion verbs that distinguish one-time actions from habitual ones, and movement on foot from movement by vehicle. 'Jít' = to go (one-time, on foot), 'chodit' = to go (habitual, on foot), 'jet' = to go/ride (one-time, by vehicle), 'jezdit' = to go/ride (habitual, by vehicle). Learn how direction adverbs like tam (there), sem (here), and dopředu (forward) combine with these verbs.

exercises.prompt: Which verb means 'to go on foot' as a ONE-TIME action (today, right now)?

data.question_cz: Které sloveso znamená 'jít pěšky' jako JEDNORÁZOVÝ děj (dnes, právě teď)?
data.question_en: Which verb means 'to go on foot' as a ONE-TIME action (today, right now)?
data.explanation: 'Jít' = to go (on foot, one-time, specific direction). Example: 'Dnes jdu do školy.' = Today I am going to school. Use 'jít' for a single trip happening now or at a specific time. 'Chodit' is the habitual version (going repeatedly).

exercises.prompt: Which verb means 'to go by vehicle' as a HABITUAL or repeated action?

data.question_cz: Které sloveso znamená 'jezdit' jako OPAKOVANÝ děj?
data.question_en: Which verb means 'to go by vehicle' as a HABITUAL or repeated action?
data.explanation: 'Jezdit' = to go/ride (by vehicle, habitual, repeated, or no specific direction). Example: 'Každý den jezdím do práce autobusem.' = Every day I ride to work by bus. Use 'jezdit' for regular trips by vehicle. 'Jet' is the one-time version.

exercises.prompt: Complete: 'Zítra ___ do Prahy vlakem.' (Tomorrow I ___ to Prague by train.)

data.question_en: Complete: 'Zítra ___ do Prahy vlakem.' (Tomorrow I ___ to Prague by train.)
data.explanation: 'Zítra jedu do Prahy vlakem.' = Tomorrow I am going to Prague by train. 'Jedu' is the 1st person singular of 'jet' — a one-time trip by vehicle. 'Vlakem' (by train) confirms vehicle travel. We use 'jet' (one-time) not 'jezdit' (habitual) because tomorrow is a single specific trip.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'jít' or 'chodit': 'Every morning I ___ to school on foot.'

data.explanation: 'Každé ráno chodím do školy pěšky.' = Every morning I go to school on foot. 'Každé ráno' (every morning) signals a habitual/repeated action, so we use 'chodit' → 'chodím' (1st person singular). 'Pěšky' (on foot) confirms walking. Use 'chodit' for regular, repeated trips on foot.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'jet' or 'jezdit': 'Right now we ___ to Brno by car.'

data.explanation: 'Právě teď jedeme do Brna autem.' = Right now we are going to Brno by car. 'Právě teď' (right now) signals a one-time action, so we use 'jet' → 'jedeme' (1st person plural). 'Autem' (by car) confirms vehicle travel. Use 'jet' for a single, specific trip by vehicle happening now.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I am going to the cinema now.' (on foot, one-time)

data.source: I am going to the cinema now.
data.grammar_note: 'Teď jdu do kina.' = I am going to the cinema now. 'Jdu' = I am going (1st person singular of 'jít' — one-time, on foot). 'Teď' = now. 'Do kina' = to the cinema. We use 'jít' (not 'chodit') because it's a single trip happening right now. Walking to a nearby cinema is assumed to be on foot.

exercises.prompt: Translate to English: 'O víkendech jezdíme na chatu autem.'

data.source: O víkendech jezdíme na chatu autem.
accepted_answers: On weekends we go to the cottage by car.
We go to the cottage by car on weekends.
On weekends we drive to the cottage by car.
data.grammar_note: 'O víkendech' = on weekends (habitual/repeated). 'Jezdíme' = we go/ride (1st person plural of 'jezdit' — habitual, by vehicle). 'Na chatu' = to the cottage. 'Autem' = by car. The habitual marker 'o víkendech' (every weekend) requires 'jezdit', not 'jet'.

exercises.answer_key: On weekends we go to the cottage by car.

exercises.prompt: Match each Czech motion verb to its English meaning.

data.prompt_en: Match each Czech motion verb to its English meaning (mode and frequency).
data.explanation: Czech motion verbs encode two distinctions: (1) on foot (jít/chodit) vs by vehicle (jet/jezdit), and (2) one-time/specific (jít/jet) vs habitual/repeated (chodit/jezdit). This four-way system is fundamental to Czech. Example: 'jdu' (I'm going now, on foot) vs 'chodím' (I go regularly, on foot) vs 'jedu' (I'm riding now, by vehicle) vs 'jezdím' (I ride regularly, by vehicle).

exercises.answer_key: jít→to go (one-time, on foot), chodit→to go (habitual, on foot), jet→to go/ride (one-time, by vehicle), jezdit→to go/ride (habitual, by vehicle)

exercises.prompt: Match each conjugated form to the correct pronoun (forms of 'jít').

data.prompt_en: Match each conjugated form of 'jít' to the correct pronoun.
data.explanation: 'Jít' conjugation (present, one-time on foot): já jdu, ty jdeš, on/ona jde, my jdeme, vy jdete, oni jdou. Note the stem 'jd-'. Compare with 'chodit' (habitual): chodím, chodíš, chodí, chodíme, chodíte, chodí — note different endings (-í type).

exercises.prompt: Find the mistake: the wrong motion verb is used.

data.prompt_en: Find the mistake — the wrong motion verb is used (one-time vs habitual).
data.sentence_cz: Každý den jdu do práce autobusem.
data.correct_sentence_cz: Každý den jezdím do práce autobusem.
data.explanation: 'Každý den' (every day) signals a habitual, repeated action. For repeated travel by vehicle, use 'jezdit' → 'jezdím' (1st person singular). 'Jdu' is from 'jít' (one-time, on foot) — wrong on both counts: it's not one-time, and 'autobusem' (by bus) is a vehicle. Correct: 'Každý den jezdím do práce autobusem.' = Every day I ride to work by bus.

exercises.answer_key: Každý den jezdím do práce autobusem.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form: 'I am going there on foot now.'

data.explanation: 'Teď jdu tam pěšky.' = I am going there on foot now. 'Teď' = now, 'jdu' = I am going (jít, 1st sg.), 'tam' = there (direction adverb), 'pěšky' = on foot. Direction adverbs like 'tam' (there), 'sem' (here), 'domů' (home) follow the verb. 'Pěšky' (on foot) typically comes at the end.

exercises.prompt: Pronounce this sentence using one-time and habitual motion verbs for contrast.

data.target_text: Dnes jdu do školy pěšky, ale obvykle jezdím autobusem.
data.translation_en: Today I am going to school on foot, but usually I ride by bus.

exercises.answer_key: Dnes jdu do školy pěšky, ale obvykle jezdím autobusem.
